informal: Suppose that $f$ is holomorphic in an open set $\Omega$. Prove that if $\text{Im}(f)$ is constant, then $f$ is constant.
lean:
theorem Shakarchi_exercise_1_13b_corrected {f : ℂ → ℂ} {Ω : Set ℂ}
    (hΩ : IsOpen Ω) (hConn : IsPreconnected Ω)
    (hf : DifferentiableOn ℂ f Ω)
    (hc : ∃ c : ℝ, ∀ z ∈ Ω, (f z).im = c) :
    ∀ a b : Ω, f a = f b
reasoning: The conclusion `∀ a b : Ω, f a = f b` faithfully encodes "f is constant on Ω", and the hypothesis `(f z).im = c` correctly captures "Im(f) is constant on Ω". However, the Lean version adds an extra hypothesis `IsPreconnected Ω` that is not present in the informal statement (the informal only says "open set Ω", not "region"). The author's `_corrected` suffix even signals this was added. Strictly speaking, this makes the Lean theorem a special case of the literal informal claim (which is actually false without connectedness, but the literal text does not include it), so the Lean statement is logically weaker — it is implied by the informal claim but does not imply the unrestricted version.
